data.title: Clough closed-end funds announce renewal of share repurchase programs
data.description:
The Boards of Trustees for several Clough Capital Partners L.P. closed-end funds have renewed their share repurchase programs, allowing each fund to buy back up to 5% of outstanding common shares in the open market through June 30, 2026.

 
  • Clough Global Equity Fund (NYSE:GLQ)
  • Clough Global Opportunities Fund (NYSE:GLO)
  • Clough Global Dividend & Income Fund (NYSE:GLV)

The share repurchases were originally approved in June 2023 and have been renewed annually thereafter.

data.title: Europe swings lower on rising tensions in the Middle East
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.00am ET.

London (UKX) -0.45% to 8,844.

Germany (DAX:IND) -1.48% to 23,418, Germany inflation flat at 2.1% in May. Wholesale prices in Germany increased 0.4% Y/Y in May.

France (CAC:IND) -1.17% to 7,674, France inflation drops to 0.7% in May.

Spain's annual inflation rate eases to 2% in May.

Industrial production in Hungary fell by 5.0% Y/Y in April.

The annual inflation rate in Finland stood at 0.5% in May.

Sweden’s annual inflation rate eased to 0.2% in May.

The Netherlands’ trade surplus narrowed to €9.14B in April.

Euro Area records trade surplus of €9.9B in April.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) dropped 0.85% to 545.81, on track for a fifth straight day in red, as escalating Middle East tensions turn investor sentiment. Israel’s strike targeting Iran's nuclear facilities took markets by surprise. Iran responded with drone attacks after confirming multiple fatalities.

To note, the Euro Stoxx 50 was earlier down 1.7% and the Stoxx 600 off 1.2% in Europe's premarket trade. European natural gas futures rose nearly 3%, the highest level since early April, tracking a broader surge in energy markets.

The rising tensions in the Middle-East gave a push to oil prices, while travel and leisure sector suffered. Air carriers suffered losses on the catastrophic crash involving an Air India Boeing (BA) 787-8 destined for Gatwick, UK. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 2 basis points to 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis points to 2.49%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points to 4.53%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Dundee Precious Metals to buy Adriatic Metals in $1.3B deal
data.description:
Canada's Dundee Precious Metals (OTCPK:DPMLF) said Friday it agreed to acquire U.K.-based Adriatic Metals (OTCPK:ADMLF) in a cash-and-stock deal valued at $1.3B.

Toronto-listed Dundee (OTCPK:DPMLF) is offering 268 pence per Adriatic (OTCPK:ADMLF) share, consisting of 93 pence in cash and 0.1590 new Dundee shares for each Adriatic share, representing a ~50% premium to Adriatic’s closing price of 177.8 pence on May 19, the last trading day before it confirmed takeover talks with Dundee.

The acquisition would give Dundee (OTCPK:DPMLF) full ownership of Adriatic’s (OTCPK:ADMLF) Vares silver project in Bosnia and Herzegovina, which it said should help it boost production to as much as 425K gold equivalent oz by 2027.

If the deal is completed, Dundee (OTCPK:DPMLF) shareholders would own ~75% and former Adriatic (OTCPK:ADMLF) shareholders would own ~25% of Dundee's enlarged issued share capital.

data.title: Citizens Financial increases stock buyback authorization to $1.5B
data.description:
Financial institution Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG) announced on Friday an increase in its share repurchase authorization to $1.5B.

Shares were -1.24% pre-market to $40.66.

The company had announced a $1.25B stock buyback program in June last year. A $300M of capacity is remaining under the prior authorization.

The repurchases may be executed in the open market or in privately negotiated transactions.

data.title: FB Financial, Southern States Bancshares get regulatory approvals for merger
data.description:
Bank holding companies FB Financial (NYSE:FBK) and Southern States Bancshares (NASDAQ:SSBK) announced on Thursday receipt of regulatory approvals to complete the proposed merger.

The two companies jointly reported their entry into a definitive merger agreement in March under which Southern States will be merged with and into FB Financial.

The transaction is expected to close on July 1.

data.title: Cloudflare prices $1.75B convertible notes offering
data.description:
Cloudflare (NYSE:NET) has priced its private offering of $1.75B aggregate principal amount of Convertible Senior Notes due 2030.

The company also granted the initial purchasers of the notes a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $250M aggregate principal amount of the notes solely to cover over-allotments.

The notes will not bear regular interest will mature on June 15, 2030, unless earlier redeemed, repurchased, or converted in accordance with their terms.

The notes will be convertible at an initial conversion rate of 4.0376 shares of Cloudflare’s Class A common stock per $1,000 principal amount of notes, equivalent to an initial conversion price of ~$247.67 a share, which represents a conversion premium of ~45% to Cloudflare’s last reported sale price.

The sale is expected to settle on June 17, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions, and result in approximately $1.72B in net proceeds (assuming no exercise of the initial purchasers’ option to purchase additional notes).

Cloudflare (NYSE:NET) intends to use the proceeds from the offering to pay the cost of the capped call transactions and any remaining proceeds for general corporate purposes.

NET -2.78% premarket.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Hyperscale Data subsidiary Sentinum anticipates annual bitcoin mining run rate of ~$41 million
data.description:
• Hyperscale Data (NYSE:GPUS) Friday said that its wholly owned subsidiary, Sentinum, expects that starting in July 2025, it will be mining an aggregate of approximately 375.24 Bitcoin per year.
  • Such operations would represent a current Bitcoin mining annual run rate of approximately $41 million worth of Bitcoin.
  • The mining run rate is based upon a recent Bitcoin price of approximately $108,000.
